negativeEvidence.strongNegativeEvidence: Main UCSF SOM hub explicitly excludes international (non-LCME) students from clinical experiences. The international lane lives at UCSF Fresno (a sibling campus), not at the main UCSF Medical Center.
negativeEvidence.weakNegativeEvidence: Main UCSF SOM hub does not name UCSF Medical Center (hospital) explicitly as a placement site; placement is implicit through SOM department electives. Visa language absent from both lanes.

nextAction: For public-safe activation: (a) SCHOOL_LEVEL_SOURCE — UCSF SOM hub, US LCME/COCA AACOM only, international explicitly excluded at MAIN UCSF; (b) $300 fee verbatim ($550 Canadian); (c) max 12 weeks; (d) VSLO required. UCSF Fresno is a separate sibling campus with its own international lane ($1,040/4wk) — that gets its own packet in a future sprint; the active runtime UCSF entry must NOT claim to cover the Fresno international lane.
plainEnglishSummary: UCSF's main visiting student program accepts only US LCME and COCA-accredited students; international medical students are explicitly excluded. Applications go through VSLO with a $300 processing fee ($550 for Canadian schools). Max 12 weeks. The UCSF Medical Center hospital is the implicit placement site. UCSF Fresno is a SEPARATE sibling campus that has its own international visiting students lane at $1,040 per 4 weeks plus housing — this is not the same as the main UCSF Medical Center listing currently in active runtime.
driftCheck: This advanced national USCE discovery because UCSF main hub policy was captured verbatim with the explicit international-exclusion language; an important new finding (Fresno = separate international-eligible sibling campus) was recorded as a follow-up packet candidate rather than merged into the main entry.
